questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Anhydride is formed when two molecules of carboxylic acid react. Saponification is a process in which alkaline hydrolysis of esters forms a very important cleansing product and cosmetics. The process forms an alcohol derivative and a carboxylate ion that will be protonated further by a metal to form a salt (soap).

Vegetable oils and animal fats are the traditional materials that are saponified. These greasy materials, triesters called triglycerides, are usually mixtures derived from diverse fatty acids. In the traditional saponification, the triglyceride is treated with lye, which cleaves the ester bonds, releasing fatty acid salts (soaps) and glycerol. The saponification process is widely used in the production of soaps, which are essential cleansing products and cosmetics. During saponification, ester reacts with an inorganic base to produce alcohol and soap.Soaps are just potassium or sodium salts of long-chain fatty acids.They are widely used in bathing, cleaning, washing and in other household chores. The mechanism of saponification follows the nucleophilic carbonyl substitution pathway. In this pathway, the hydroxide ion acts as a nucleophile and attacks the carbonyl carbon of the ester molecule. This results in the formation of a tetrahedral intermediate which then collapses to form a carboxylate ion and an alcohol molecule. The process forms an alcohol derivative and a carboxylate ion that will be protonated further by a metal to form a salt (soap). Saponification is a process of cleaving esters into carboxylate salts and alcohols by the action of aqueous alkali. questions.text: Which of these in aq.sol of equal concentration has the lowest pH?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chloroethanoic acid is also known as monochloroacetic acid. It contains a chlorine atom (Cl) attached to the carbon atom of the carboxylic acid group. Due to the electronegativity of chlorine, it enhances the acidity of the compound, making it a strong acid. In an aqueous solution of equal concentration, it will have the lowest pH because it readily donates.

Ethanoic acid is commonly known as acetic acid and is found in vinegar. It contains two carbon atoms in its structure. It's a weak acid compared to chloroethanoic acid because it lacks the electronegative chlorine atom. In an aqueous solution of equal concentration, it will have a higher pH (less acidic) than chloroethanoic acid.

Ethylamine is an organic compound containing two carbon atoms. It is a weak base rather than an acid. In an aqueous solution of equal concentration, it will have a higher pH (more basic) compared to both acids.

Phenol is an aromatic compound containing a benzene ring with a hydroxyl (OH) group attached. It is a weak acid, but its acidity is lower than that of chloroethanoic acid. In an aqueous solution of equal concentration, it will have a higher pH (less acidic) than chloroethanoic acid but lower than ethanoic acid.

questions.text: By product produced during hydrolysis of alkane nitrile in basic medium is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The by-product produced during the hydrolysis of alkane nitrile in a basic medium is ammonia (NH3).When an alkane nitrile is hydrolyzed in a basic medium, the nitrogen atom is converted to an ammonium ion (NH4+). The ammonium ion is then neutralized by a hydroxide ion (OH-) to form ammonia gas (NH3).Here are the steps of the reaction: The nitrogen atom in the alkane nitrile is attracted to the hydroxide ion (OH-) from the base.The hydroxide ion abstracts a proton from the nitrogen atom.As a result, the nitrogen atom becomes positively charged and is converted to an ammonium ion (NH4+) . The ammonium ion is then neutralized by another hydroxide ion (OH-) from the base. The neutralization reaction produces water (H2O) and ammonia gas (NH3). The overall reaction of the hydrolysis of alkane nitrile in a basic medium is: R-CN + H2O → R-COOH + NH3 Where R is an alkyl group.
Ammonium sulfate is formed when the ammonium ion (NH4+) from the hydrolysis of the alkane nitrile reacts with the sulfate ion (SO42-) from the base.
Ammonium chloride is formed when the ammonium ion (NH4+) from the hydrolysis of the alkane nitrile reacts with the chloride ion (Cl-) from the base.

Ammonium nitrate (NH4NO3) is not a by-product produced during hydrolysis of alkane nitrile in a basic medium. Ammonium nitrate is a white, crystalline solid that is used as an agricultural fertilizer and in explosives.

questions.text: The highest melting point is observed by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Butanoic acid, also known as butyric acid, has a chemical structure with a chain of four carbon atoms. While it has a higher molecular weight than Propanoic acid (option 3), it has a lower molecular weight than Pentanoic acid (option 2). As the carbon chain is shorter than Pentanoic acid, the Van der Waals forces between molecules are weaker. Weaker intermolecular forces result in a lower melting point compared to Pentanoic acid.

The highest melting point is observed in option 2) Pentanoic acid. Pentanoic acid (also known as valeric acid) has a longer carbon chain compared to the other options, which are Butanoic acid (option 1) and Propanoic acid (option 3). The longer carbon chain in Pentanoic acid results in stronger intermolecular forces (Van der Waals forces) between its molecules. These stronger forces require more energy to break, leading to a higher melting point for Pentanoic acid compared to Butanoic acid and Propanoic acid.

Propanoic acid has a chain of three carbon atoms. Although it has a higher melting point than Butanoic acid (option 1) because of its longer chain, it still has a lower melting point than Pentanoic acid (option 2). The longer carbon chain in Propanoic acid increases the Van der Waals forces compared to Butanoic acid, but it is not long enough to surpass the stronger intermolecular forces present in Pentanoic acid.

Unlike organic acids, hydrochloric acid is a simple inorganic molecule with the formula HCl. It exists as a gas at room temperature and atmospheric pressure, so it doesn't have a melting point. The melting point is the temperature at which a solid substance changes into a liquid state. Since HCl is a gas under typical room conditions, it undergoes sublimation where it directly converts from a solid to a gaseous state when heated.
